stemHtml:
Based on the published NOTAM reporting GNSS signal interruptions in the Nicosia FIR, how should a pilot plan the use of GNSS-based procedures for a flight within the affected period? 

A1014/23 NOTAMR A0473/23 Q) LCCC/QGWXX/IV/NBO/E/000/999/3426N03246E171 A) LCCC B) 2307030723 C) 2310022359 E) GNSS SIGNAL INTERRUPTIONS HAVE BEEN REPORTED WITHIN NICOSIA FIR. PILOTS ARE REQUESTED TO REPORT TO ATC. WITHIN TMA CONVENTIONAL INSTRUMENT PROCEDURES ARE AVAILABLE ON PILOT REQUEST

options.a: Pilot may use GNSS-based procedures without restrictions as ATC will have a record of when any signal outages are planned to occur.
options.b: Cannot use GNSS for position referencing since the signals are unreliable and there are no backup alternatives in the event of loss of GNSS signals.
options.c: Pilot may plan to use GNSS-based procedures but must consider contingency plans in the event of any issues with the GNSS signals.
options.d: Cannot plan to use any GNSS-based procedure as there are planned outages of GNSS necessitating the use of conventional instrument procedures.
